rationale: The citing paper explicitly positions itself as extending prior promotion-decomposition models that treated consumption as fixed, while the cited paper is a directly aligned 2007 JMR study on decomposing promotion-induced stockpiling effects with household-level modeling. Given the near-identical problem domain (promotion-bump decomposition into switching/stockpiling/consumption components) and methodological continuity, this is likely a substantive foundational/comparison reference rather than an incidental mention.
